message:
perf(context): add Aho-Corasick quick-reject to sanitize_for_pattern_matching

Add a pre-tokenization check using Aho-Corasick to skip expensive
tokenization when no safe-registry commands appear in the input.
This significantly improves performance for large inputs like heredocs
that don't invoke any of the masked commands (git, grep, echo, etc.).

The optimization works by checking if any of the safe command names
(echo, printf, git, grep, rg, ag, ack, gh, curl, jq, docker, kubectl,
xargs, cargo, npm, command) appear in the input string. If none are
found, tokenization is skipped entirely and the original string is
returned.

Fixes benchmark regression on medium_heredoc (~50% improvement).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
